docData.title: Substance Use Disorder Foundations
docData.subtitle: Audio-guided slide lesson for learner playback
docData.description: Firestore-ready example aligned with the current course schema and the slideDeck block used by the manager builder and learner player.

blocks.html: This lesson introduces the care setting, the patient journey and the responsibilities of the care team.

slides.title: Why this training matters
slides.transcript: Welcome to the training. In this first slide, we explain why standardized substance use disorder education matters for patient safety and continuity of care.
slides.notesHtml: Key point: Every learner should understand the basics of screening, response and escalation pathways.

interactiveCards.title: Patient safety impact
interactiveCards.bodyHtml: Consistent screening and escalation reduce missed warning signs and improve handoff quality.

interactiveCards.title: Flip: care team role
interactiveCards.teaser: Tap to reveal the team expectation
interactiveCards.bodyHtml: Every team member is expected to document observations and escalate according to protocol.

slides.title: Recognizing patient risk factors
slides.transcript: Risk factors may include prior misuse history, unstable access to medication, co-occurring behavioral health needs and social stressors.
slides.notesHtml:
• Review the chart before the visit
  • Watch for changes in affect and orientation
  • Document concerns promptly

interactiveCards.title: Medication history
interactiveCards.bodyHtml: Look for refill gaps, dose changes and recent emergency visits related to medication access.

interactiveCards.title: Behavioral cues
interactiveCards.teaser: Hotspot near the patient interaction zone
interactiveCards.bodyHtml: Changes in affect, confusion and withdrawal can signal increased risk and need for escalation.

slides.title: Coordinating the response
slides.transcript: A safe response relies on communication between the clinician, supervisor, care coordinator and the rest of the multidisciplinary team.
slides.notesHtml: Document observations, follow internal protocol and escalate according to role-based guidance.

interactiveCards.title: Step 1: Document
interactiveCards.bodyHtml: Capture the observation clearly, objectively and in the correct system of record.

interactiveCards.title: Step 2: Notify
interactiveCards.bodyHtml: Notify the right clinician or supervisor using the escalation path defined by policy.

interactiveCards.title: Step 3: Confirm handoff
interactiveCards.bodyHtml: Confirm the handoff was received and any immediate follow-up action is assigned.

blocks.question: Which action best supports safe SUD care coordination?

choices.text: Wait until the end of the week to document concerns
Use the care protocol and escalate observations promptly
Rely only on memory rather than standardized notes

docData.accomodations: Contact your training administrator for alternative formats.
